title: Accenture: Consumers show growing trust in AI shopping agents
slides: Accenture found consumer trust in AI shopping agents rising.
Over half now say they'd let AI pick purchases.
Yet most still refuse to share payment data with it.
Brands must redesign checkout for partial delegation now.
Test removing one payment step—watch conversion shift.
thread_text:
1. Trust is shifting

Accenture found 50% more consumers now trust AI to shop—yet the real friction isn't in the pick, it's in the pay.

2. Half hand over choice

Over 50% would let AI decide what to buy—but only if checkout feels safe.

3. The payment wall

67% refuse to share card details with AI agents. Trust stops at the PIN.

4. Partial delegation works

Brands should redesign checkout for customers who delegate search but not payment. Remove friction between.

5. Test a step

Cut one payment-screen field on your site this week. Measure conversion before and after. What moves?
video_script: Consumers trust AI to pick purchases.
But they won't share payment data.
Remove one step, watch conversion shift.
Test it this week on your site.

script: Consumers trust AI to pick purchases.
But they won't share payment data.
Remove one step, watch conversion shift.
Test it this week on your site.

instagram.caption:
More than half of shoppers now trust an AI agent to pick what they buy—but 67% still refuse to share their card details with it. 🛍️

Trust flows from search to suggestion, then hits a wall at checkout. People delegate the choice, not the payment.

If you sell anything online, redesign checkout for partial delegation: let AI pick, but keep payment human.

Which single field on your payment screen could you remove this week—and what would happen to conversions? 🔍
